interaction_phrase: the joint efforts of the QuantERA Consortium partners
occurrence_sentence: Built on previous experience and the joint efforts of the QuantERA Consortium partners, as well as consultation with the EC and the QT Flagship, QuantERA has prepared this report.

interaction_phrase: supported by the national representatives of the Quantum Community Network
occurrence_sentence: In spring 2023 the QuantERA Consortium members supported by the national representatives of the Quantum Community Network completed the survey on the state of affairs within their respective countries with regard to the following:

interaction_phrase: 31 European Union member states and associated countries
occurrence_sentence: The key findings are based on the results of an inventory conducted in the spring of 2023 among 41 members of the QuantERA Consortium, drawn from 31 European Union member states and associated countries.

interaction_phrase: funded by the European Union
occurrence_sentence: On behalf of the Federal Ministry of Education, Science and Research (BMBWF) and funded by the European Union under the Development and Resilience Plan (2020-2026), the Austrian Research Promotion Agency (FFG) and the Austrian Science Fund (FWF) implement the Quantum Austria Funding Initiative9 launched for the period 2021 to 2026.

interaction_phrase: involves players from Israel, Finland and Austria
occurrence_sentence: The quantum computing international consortium involves players from Israel, Finland and Austria and its purpose is to develop open technological building blocks for a quantum full stack computer in two different technologies: (a) "ion traps" and "superconductors" (b) demonstration of the technologies on 50-qubit computers.

target_actor: National Centre of Competence in Research (NCCR)
interaction_phrase: SPIN – Spin Qubits in Silicon, a new NCCR, was launched in
occurrence_sentence: SPIN – Spin Qubits in Silicon111, a new NCCR, was launched in 2020. It is focused on quantum computing with spin qubits in silicon and germanium. For its first funding period (2020-2023), it is endowed with a total budget of about CHF 40 M of which CHF 17 M is granted by the SNSF
